name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-26
title: Extract enforcement/check-*.py + validate-feature.py logic into contract/lib/checks.py library API
description:
Architectural cleanup: separate 'shape' from 'content' in contract feature. Today contract holds 9 enforcement check scripts + validate-feature.py — each is mostly CLI shim around hard-coded logic. Move the LOGIC into a library API in contract that any caller can import; keep thin CLI shims (or drop them).

Create .claude/features/contract/lib/checks.py exposing:
  check_tests_non_interactive(feature_dir) -> CheckResult
  check_sentinel(path) -> CheckResult
  check_naming(root) -> CheckResult
  check_imports_resolve(feature_dir) -> CheckResult
  check_symlinks_resolve(root) -> CheckResult
  check_template_producer_consistency(template_path) -> CheckResult
  check_numbered_lists(root, scope) -> CheckResult
  validate_feature(feature_dir) -> CheckResult

CheckResult is a dataclass-like with .passed/.failed/.messages.

Keep the existing CLI scripts as thin wrappers that call the library (so existing callers keep working). Add unit tests for the library functions. Bump contract version.

OUT OF SCOPE for this cycle: rewiring callers (separate cycle handles that). Constants (sentinel string, rabbit-* prefix, etc.) live as module constants in checks.py for now; future cycle may consolidate into a contract constants module.
